ground_truth.title: The latest developer news & insights - The GitHub Blog
ground_truth.main_content: News & insights category page featuring the latest articles from GitHub. Featured articles include: 'The future of AI-powered software optimization (and how it can help your team)' by Paull Young discussing Continuous Efficiency; 'Let's talk about GitHub Actions' by Ben De St Paer-Gotch examining rebuilt core architecture and performance upgrades; 'The new identity of a developer: What changes and what doesn't in the AI era' by Eirini Kalliamvakou exploring how AI users are shifting from code producers to strategic orchestrators; 'Your stack, your rules: Introducing custom agents in GitHub Copilot for observability, IaC, and security' by Griffin Ashe on partner-built Copilot agents. Latest articles include GitHub Availability Report: November 2025, 'The local-first rebellion': How Home Assistant became the most important project in your house, and Octoverse 2025: The state of open source.
with: Resources for developers seeking to stay informed about the latest industry trends, research, and updates from GitHub.
We envision the future of AI-enabled tooling to look like near-effortless engineering for sustainability. We call it Continuous Efficiency.
A look at how we rebuilt GitHub Actions' core architecture and shipped long-requested upgrades to improve performance, workflow flexibility, reliability, and everyday developer experience.
Discover how advanced AI users are redefining software development—shifting from code producers to strategic orchestrators—through delegation, verification, and a new era of AI-fluent engineering.
Use partner-built Copilot agents to debug, secure, and automate engineering workflows across your terminal, editor, and github.com.
without: We do newsletters, too - Discover tips, technical guides, and best practices in our biweekly newsletter just for devs

items.alt: Illustration of a chemistry lab setup with interconnected glassware and tubing. Beakers, flasks, test tubes, and a large central reaction vessel contain glowing green and blue liquids. A pressure gauge, condenser coil, funnels, and heating plates appear along a blue tiled lab wall, with fluids flowing through tubes between the containers.
items.caption: Illustration of a chemistry lab setup with interconnected glassware and tubing
